title: T&L at Lunch Series – Duke Kunshan Center for Teaching and Learning
text:
T&L at Lunch Series
The Teaching and Learning at Lunch (T&L at Lunch) discussion series is a bi-weekly event organized by the CTL. Faculty members from all disciplines gather together over lunch to share their experiences and brainstorm effective teaching practices. It is usually initiated or co-led by faculty and the CTL.
In case you missed any of them, here are the summaries, main takeaways, and resources for the past lunch sessions (NetID log-in required).
AY 24-25
Fall 2024
Aug. 14 First day of class
Aug. 24 Enhancing Engagement in Larger Classes
Sept. 11 Engagement with Tech in and outside the classroom
Sept. 25 Navigating Grade Disputes & Appeals: Effective Strategies for Faculty
Oct. 16, Engaging Minds Through Gameful Pedagogy
Oct. 30 Cultivating DEIB in Teaching
Nov. 13 From Multidisciplinary to Interdisciplinary
Nov. 20 Teaching and Learning with the Local Community
Nov. 27 Confronting the Elephant in the Classroom: Academic Integrity
Spring 2025
Jan. 15 Learning Space Design for Classrooms
Feb. 19 Assessment in the Age of GenAI
Mar. 5 Meaningful Course Field Trips
Apr. 2 Student Perspectives on GenAI in Teaching and Learning
Apr. 23 Teaching of Essential Skills in the Age of AI
AY 23-24
Fall 2023
Aug. 16 Welcome and networking
Aug. 30 Classroom engagement with or without technology
Sept. 6 Teaching grant info session
Sept. 13 Position research in your course
Sept. 27 Teaching Writing in First-Year Writing Intensive Courses
Oct. 11 Interpret student feedback on teaching
Oct. 25 Teaching with Generative AI
Nov. 8 Teaching and Learning with the Local Community
Nov 22. Oral Examination as an Authentic Assessment
Spring 2024
Jan. 10 Parachuting onto Canvas
Jan. 24 Design Courses for the 7-week Structure
Feb. 7 Seeing Generative AI Through Students’ Eyes
Mar. 6 Flipped Classrooms at DKU
Mar. 20 Meaningful Classroom Engagement
Apr. 3 Inclusive Teaching Mindset and Practices
Apr. 17 Grading for Equity
Apr. 24 Empowering Teaching and Learning through Community-Based Learning
AY 22-23
Fall 2022
Aug. 18 The First Day of Class
Aug. 31 Student Engagement
Sept. 14 Assessment Principles and Best Practices
Sept. 28 Nurturing “Researchers” of Tomorrow
Oct. 12 Forward Together: What technology can empower in-person teaching?
Oct. 26 Flipped Classroom
Nov. 9 Alternative Grading
Nov. 23 Academic Integrity: Policies and Practice
Spring 2023
Jan. 11 Engage Students in Peer Review
Feb. 1 Rethinking Teaching and Learning with AI
Feb. 15 Facilitate Effective Group Work
Mar. 1 AI and Writing
Mar. 29 SW Mentoring
Apr. 12 Scaffolding Student Writing
Apr. 26 Office Hours
May 10 Learning Space: Optimizing Physical Classrooms for Active Learning
